summary: Excellent sound quality captures a mixed‑quality 1994 show with strong Tom Thumb's Blues, Way To Go Home and Stuck On The Moon but a shaky first set.
review: The recording is praised as crystal‑clear, letting listeners hear the nuances of the performance. While the first set falters – notably Jerry’s struggling on So Many Roads – the second half shines with a powerful Tom Thumb's Blues, a tight Way To Go Home and an inspired Stuck On The Moon. Overall it’s a decent show marred by uneven moments but preserved in superb audio.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviewers note excellent recording quality (high score) but give low‑mid ratings for the musical performance, leading to an overall moderate rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Sound quality described as excellent; clear and crisp with no reported dropouts or mix issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts promising but loses steam, especially on So Many Roads; second set recovers with strong post‑space playing and tight transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Struggles early (especially on So Many Roads) then delivers a solid performance later in the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Brings the band back together with an excellent Tom Thumb's Blues, helping lift the first set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince cranks up the organ throughout, adding texture especially when Jerry deals with rig issues.
band_member_comments.Drums: A notable drum intro on Victim or the Crime is highlighted; overall solid drumming.
